forecast-period.text: A cold front with an embedded low pressure system lies west of Kangaroo Island. The low is expected to move north and over the Eyre Peninsula tomorrow, weakening as it moves into NSW on Tuesday. A weak ridge of high pressure begins to move across SA later in the week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers across most districts, most likely across the agricultural area. Isolated thunderstorms about the central districts. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds fresh at times about the coast.

Mostly dry across the northwest, showers elsewhere with a possible thunderstorm about the south of Eyre Peninsula. Northeast to northwesterly winds, ahead of a south to southwesterly change about the west and central districts, freshening near the coasts.

Showers across most districts, less likely across the far north. Isolated thunderstorms possible over the North East Pastoral District. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds.
Isolated showers in the morning, becoming less likely during the day. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds.

text.p: Isolated showers about southern coasts and ranges. Maximum temperatures near to below average. West to southwesterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Thursday: 1 to 5 mm about the pastoral districts. Elsewhere, 5 to 15 mm across the agricultural areas and west coast, increasing to 15 to 30 mm across western Eyre Peninsula, Kangaroo Island and eastern districts.
